name: w1 6x9 plumber loss-demo v3
hypothesis: A plumber-specific loss plus a demo he can run in 40 seconds — call the demo line, hear his own excuse in his customer's ear, and watch the AI save the job — outdraws copy that asserts the fix works without letting him verify it.

notes.parent: w1 6x9 plumber loss-demo v2 (55cb6586) — frozen by its proof wave. Layout inherited unchanged.
notes.change_vs_parent: ONE change: the CTA moves from the SALES line (888-853-8575) to the PRODUCT DEMO line (424-407-1682). Same left-column layout, same URL, same offer. Texting the sales line reached an AI salesperson — it demonstrated that an AI exists, not that it books HIS jobs, so "it does the same for your callers" was a CLAIM he had to take on faith. Claim #2 says faith is exactly what does not convert. The 424 runs a booking-trade AI, so he experiences the product doing its actual job to him. Canonical: docs/reference-toll-free-inventory.md — "Demo promise = watch it book the job — needs a booking-trade AI, NOT the sales AI".

grounding.'Your competitor picked up.' (the loss f: CONFIRMED — claim #1. A loss (someone took the job) M=3.48 vs non-gain M=2.65 vs gain M=2.49; rips-irsp.com/articles/10.5334/irsp.15. This is the driver of the card.
grounding.'Their water heater just burst.' (trade: CONFIRMED-adjacent — claim #1's framing x personal-relevance interaction (t(222)=4.79, p=.001): the loss advantage grows with personal relevance. Trade specificity buys that relevance TRUTHFULLY and for free. This is the deliberate replacement for the unsourced $400 in w1 6x9 loss-math, which was reaching for the same amplifier by inventing a number.
grounding.'You were under a house.' (blameless): JUDGMENT, not evidence. He was working, not negligent — removes the accusation that would make him discard the card. No claim support; kept because it costs nothing and the loss frame still carries.
grounding.phone over QR: Claim #4 says QR/URL is 82% of MARKETER tracking behavior — NOT evidence this audience prefers QR. Phone/text prominence is the standing bet. No QR is printed (the pipeline has no per-piece QR generation anyway).
grounding.6x9 / 54pt / 18pt body: CONFIRMED — claim #3 (14pt body recommended, 12pt floor, generous leading) for 50+ readers; a dominant phone number does not fit 4x6. decisions.md 2026-07-12. Caveat (PMC 9376262): legibility gains stop at a critical size.
grounding.'30-day free trial. Five minutes to set: Canonical offer language — must stay aligned with every other surface (main repo commit 63f3f89 aligned hard-stop quota / 5-min setup / 30-day trial). If the offer changes anywhere, this card is a surface that changes too.
grounding.landing URL = /plumbers/spring2026a/: DELIBERATE. The plumber-designed video lives ONLY there; getnevermisscall.com/ root serves a different video (verified: different md5) and generic copy. Both pages capture ?r= via identical last-touch code, so attribution is preserved either way. Pointing at root would have wasted the plumber video.
grounding.landing URL = /p (short redirect): REPLACES the v1 note. /p 302-redirects to /plumbers/spring2026a/ preserving ?r= (verified: curl -> location: /plumbers/spring2026a/?r=crzzdqwlbx -> 200). 302 not 301 on purpose: cards are physical and live for months, so /p must stay repointable at the current campaign; a cached 301 would pin it to spring2026a in readers' browsers forever. Keeps the plumber-specific video, which root would have wasted.
grounding.'Say yes when it asks.' (the true mechan: VERIFIED against booking-system/src/i18n/messages.ts, not the docs. A non-sales line does NOT "let it ring" and does NOT go unanswered: NMC answers and plays ivr.greeting via <Gather> — "Hi, you've reached {companyName}. Our AI can text you right back... We're with our customers right now, so if you'd like a text, just say yes or press 1..." — then ivr.thankYou, hangup, SMS. BOTH docs were wrong: the inventory doc says "call, let it ring", and w1 6x9 demo-first prints "We won't pick up". Without the say-yes instruction he hangs up mid-greeting thinking he reached a machine, and the demo — the entire efficacy mediator — silently fails.
grounding.demo line company_name = \"Anytown Plumb: Changed in PROD 2026-07-15 (was "Test Plumbing"; cus_01KPPW3HRC5R8HFZK0ARY2431W, dry-run then COMMIT, 1 row). "Test" announced unfinished software in the first three words of the one asset whose whole job is response-efficacy. Name chosen by collision-checking candidates against the CSLB list itself: "Anytown" = 0 of 84,072, while Redwood/Harbor/Ironwood/Copper Creek all match REAL licensed contractors who are ON THIS MAILING LIST — mail them a card, they call the demo, and hear their own company name. A plausible name was the dangerous option, not the safe one.
grounding.front + demo are the same story from two: JUDGMENT, not evidence. The front says "you were under a house"; the IVR says "we're with our customers right now". He hears his own excuse from the customer's side, then watches the AI catch the job instead of losing it. Claim #2's mediator delivered rather than asserted.
grounding.the URL is the only bridge from demo to: VERIFIED structurally 2026-07-16: closed intent taxonomy (intent-classifier.service.ts:18), plumber.md forbids fabrication, no per-business freeform prompt field — the demo AI CANNOT pitch NMC; a caller who breaks script ("how do I get this?") gets steered back to booking. Mitigation is operational, not copy: daily 424 log check during the response window + personal founder text-back (decisions.md 2026-07-16). Escape-hatch prompt (plumber-demo trade) deferred; revisit on wave-1 transcripts.

notes.scope_limit: This card is NOT a test arm and its result is NOT a comparison. At ~500-piece waves and single-digit response (~15 responses), no A/B design — staggered or simultaneous — is statistically readable; resolving even a 50% lift needs hundreds of responses per arm. Wave 1 answers qualitative questions only: did anyone respond, what did they say, did attribution fire end-to-end. Do NOT grade loss framing, trade specificity, or the text-demo against this card's numbers. If it underperforms, that is not evidence any of the three failed.
rejected: QR code to the video — no per-piece QR generation exists, so a static QR carries no mailer code and every scan lands as an orphan (goal #1 breaks). Claim #4 also warns the 82% QR stat is MARKETER behavior, not evidence this audience prefers it, and "QR-to-SMS converts 3-5x" is in the refuted-lore list. A QR is also just a website visit with extra steps — the demo line is the only path that never leaves the phone.

The sales line (888-853-8575) as a second number — two numbers kills the phone prominence claim #4 argues for. The landing page already carries the sales number for anyone wanting a human. RE-LITIGATED 2026-07-16, rejection upheld: a second number competes with the demo (some readers call sales INSTEAD of running it — the v2 weakness back on the card), and the phone-native purchase path already exists post-demo via the SMS thread + daily founder text-back. REVISIT TRIGGER: wave-1 shows a demo-to-URL dropoff — hand-matched 424 callers who never appear as ?r= visits, or transcripts asking "how do I sign up" then stalling. If so, wave-2 fixes in order: (1) send the buy link in the personal text-back (zero card change), (2) test a small-type "Prefer to talk? (888) 853-8575" line BELOW the URL, subordinated so it can't compete with the demo CTA.
Price ($99/mo Solo) — the landing page's job. The 30-day trial is the risk-killer here.
Stats ("50% hire whoever responds first") — knowledgebase copy of unverified provenance. The front delivers the loss viscerally with no number to argue with.
A dollar figure ($400) — unsourced; trade specificity buys the same relevance truthfully.
'Every missed call is a job your competitor got' — 'Every' is falsifiable from his own memory.
'We won't pick up' / 'let it ring' — FALSE for every line; NMC answers with a consent IVR.
4x6 — 14pt body + dominant phone do not fit (decisions.md 2026-07-12).
unverified_before_drop: RESOLVED 2026-07-15 (was BLOCKING — demo calls invisible to the spine, no NmcFeed): decisions.md — ship anyway, hand-match phone responders from Twilio, accept the undercount; tightened 2026-07-16 to a DAILY log check during the response window. The readout must still record the blind spot: URL-only numbers understate response by design.

Operator to confirm by calling 424-407-1682 that the IVR now says "Anytown Plumbing" — and in the same call, play the interested plumber ("how do I get this for my company?") and record what the AI does. The cul-de-sac decision (decisions.md 2026-07-16) assumes a polite steer-back; observe it, don't trust it.
The other five 6x9 backs (ghost-6x9-a1/a2, loss-math, demo-first, jobsite-voice) still use the full-width layout that the v1 proof showed Lob truncates. None has been proofed.
